blenderReference.authoredLightScaleReason: The isolated reference rig places and sizes its Area lights from the object radius, but Blender Area energy is total power. Scaling the 1000/500 W pair by 28 compensates for this asset's 58.49-unit radius relative to the 11.06-unit N03D calibration target.

browserContract.front: The col point attribute feeds the Principled base color without an invented brightness band.
browserContract.proceduralField: Generated coordinates are normalized with Blender's evaluated object bounds, mapped by 85.09765625, and evaluated through the extracted 3D White Noise plus distorted Z Wave topology. The Rec.709 color-to-float conversion and 0.8409091234207153 mix factor are preserved.
browserContract.roughness: The extracted procedural field drives the Principled roughness range from 1.0 to 0.50048828125.
browserContract.bump: The Wave-derived height range 0.98974609375 to 1.126708984375 is converted to a derivative normal perturbation using the current interpolated normal.
browserContract.coat: The same procedural field drives coat weight from -2.08837890625 to 1.2119140625 and coat roughness from 0.7176513671875 to 0.10000000149.
browserContract.back: Geometry Backfacing selects the extracted dark-HSV emission color and its Screen/Window diagonal Wave mask; only the narrow thresholded bands become black.
browserContract.removedApproximation: The former generated-Z sine approximation has been replaced by the extracted White Noise, distorted Wave, color-to-float, Bump, roughness, coat, and backface branches.

remaining: The Bevel For Eevee group's ambient-occlusion-assisted base normal remains separate from the implemented derivative Bump, and Three.js still uses its renderer-defined clearcoat Fresnel instead of Blender's extracted coat IOR 1.59000015. The stage-one image comparison is now durable; renderer-energy calibration and those two shader semantics remain pending.

webglComparison.status: durable current implementation capture compared against the authored Blender reference

stageOneCapture.status: The WebGL material visibly restores the dense layer-line relief. The committed capture now has quantitative evidence: 0.9923 silhouette IoU, 0.0569 macro luminance MAE, 0.823 macro correlation, and 0.0621 pixel luminance MAE.

interpretation: The 0.9923 silhouette IoU confirms camera and exact geometry alignment. Stage one reproduces the authored Generated-coordinate White Noise/Wave field, derivative relief, dynamic roughness and coat fields, and patterned dark backface emission. Relative to the superseded pre-stage-one capture, pixel luminance MAE improves from 0.0668 to 0.0621 and macro MAE from 0.0628 to 0.0569, while the procedural phase/renderer differences reduce pixel correlation from 0.815 to 0.718. The remaining visual residual is localized to the Bevel For Eevee base normal, exact coat Fresnel/IOR, renderer-energy calibration, and high-frequency procedural phase/filtering.
